original: Yogi the bear becomes the first creature ejected at supersonic speeds by the US military during testing of ejection seats
Elon Musk's Neuralink company posts a video of a patient playing chess online using a brain implant
Bangladeshi woman with two uteruses safely gives birth to twins 26 days after giving birth to another child
European Space Agency reveals new data indicating that the universe is 13.82 billion years old
Swiss aviator Bertrand Piccard and British aviator Brian Jones become the first to circumnavigate the Earth in a hot air balloon.

Wayne Gretzkyties Gordie Howe's NHL record of 801 goals
Only known typo on Time Magazine cover (control=contol), all recalled
Alcatraz prison in San Francisco Bay is closed
Marit Bjørgen, Norwegian cross-country skier, 15 Olympic medals, 18 World Championship medals, born in Trondheim, Norway

Jace Norman, American actor (Henry Danger), born in Corrales, New Mexico
Suraj Sharma, Indian actor (Life of Pi, God Friended Me), born in New Delhi, India
Forrest Mars Sr., American candy magnate (Mars Inc.) who introduced the Milky Way, Mars bar, M&M's and Uncle Ben's Rice, born in Wadena, Minnesota (d. 1999)

Part of Central Park in New York City is named "Strawberry Fields" honoringJohn Lennon

Kristen Anderson-Lopez, American songwriter (Frozen), born in New York City

Ethiopia abolishes its monarchy after 3,000 years
Quebec Nordiques goaltender Ron Tugnutt sets NHL modern-day record for most saves in a regular season non-loss game (70 of 73 shots in 3-3 tie with the Boston Bruins
Antonia Maury, American astronomer (created own stellar classification, one of the Harvard 'Observatory human computers), born in Cold Spring, New York (d. 1952)
Kenny Washington signs with Rams, 1st black NFLer since 1933
Dutch Resistance fighter Hannie Schaft arrested by Nazi police
Beatles' single "She Loves You" goes #1 in the US & stays #1 for 2 weeks
Movie "Annie" directed by John Huston, starring Aileen Quinn,Albert FinneyandCarol Burnettpremieres
Deryck Whibley, Canadian guitarist and singer (Sum41 - "Fat Lip"), born in Scarborough, Ontario

Jerry Pate celebrates golf win by jumping into the water hazard
Kania skates ladies world record 500 m (39.52 sec) & 3 km (4:18.02)
US Ranger 9 launched; takes 5,814 pictures before lunar impact
Dave Patterson and John Hennessy win the Turing Award for their work on RISC computer chips
Dirk Frimout, Belgian cosmonaut and first Belgian astronaut (STS-45 in 1992), born in Poperinge, Belgium
Joe Sutter, American engineer and head of the Boeing 747 program, born in Seattle, Washington (d. 2016)
Pakistan scores 6-264 to overhaul NZ in exciting World Cup cricket semi
NBA record 106 fouls & 12 players foul out (Boston-Syracuse)
Vermont seasonal snowfall totals 132.2"
178 wildfires have burnt more than 108,000 acres across southwest Texas according to local fire officials with 23 still active

Major power outage caused by a fire at a nearby electricity substation forces Heathrow Airport, London, to shut down completely, stopping more than 1,300 flights and disrupting more than 270,000 passenger journeys
Aaron Hill, American baseball player (1st to win Silver Slugger Award in NL and AL), born in Visalia, California
Loretta Walsh becomes US Navy's 1st female Petty Officer
United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) enters into force after being ratified by the required number of nations
Governor of Missouri Mike Parson declares a state of emergency as flooding from Nebraska and Iowa flows downstream into the state
1st sea test of Trident 2 missile self-destructs, Cape Canaveral, Florida

20,000 people attempt to attend the first-ever rock & roll concert when Alan Freed presents "The Moondog Coronation Ball" at the old 12,000-seat Cleveland Arena; performers include Paul Williams and the Hucklebuckers,Tiny Grimesand the Rocking Highlanders, The Dominoes, and Varetta Dillard

Henry of Monmouth, Prince of Wales, becomes KingHenry Vof England upon the death of his father

African trade deal agreed by 44 African countries in Kigali, Rwanda

Francis Lewis, Welsh-American merchant and US Founding Father (signed the Declaration of Independence), born in Llandaff, Wales (d. 1802)
